title: The neighbour's piano
summary: For two years, a young pianist and her tired neighbour heard each other through the wall. They finally meet.

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: Excuse me. Are you the girl who plays the piano?

body.speaker: Hoa
body.text: Yes. I am very sorry. Was I too loud?

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: No. I came to ask a question.

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: Two years ago, you played a sad piece every Friday night. Then you stopped. Why?

body.speaker: Hoa
body.text: It was a piece my grandmother loved. She died that summer. I played it every Friday for one year. Then I stopped.

body.speaker: Hoa
body.text: Why did you want to know?

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: My husband was very ill that year. We could not sleep. On Friday nights, your piece was the only quiet thing in our flat. He waited for it.

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: When you stopped, he asked me, 'Why did the girl stop?' I said, 'I do not know.' He died in October.

body.speaker: Mrs Phuong
body.text: Hoa, you do not need to play it again. I only wanted to tell you. The walls are thin. We heard you. He was not alone on Friday nights.

body.speaker: Hoa
body.text: Mrs Phuong, can I ask one thing?

body.speaker: Hoa
body.text: Could you come in for a moment? I will play it for you. Just once.

annotations.meaningVi: bản nhạc / tác phẩm âm nhạc
chờ đợi nó (mong mỏi điều gì đó đến)
thứ yên bình duy nhất / thứ duy nhất mang lại sự tĩnh lặng
bạn không cần phải (làm gì đó)

annotations.phrase: I only wanted to tell you
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi chỉ muốn nói với bạn (không có yêu cầu gì thêm)
vào trong một lúc / ghé vào một chút
căn hộ (cách dùng của người Anh/Anh-Á, tương đương 'apartment')
bản nhạc buồn (colocation: tính từ 'sad' mô tả thể loại bản nhạc)

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for parallel timelines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Bài dùng quá khứ đơn để kể hai chuyện xảy ra cùng lúc nhưng ở hai phía của bức tường: 'You played a sad piece every Friday.' / 'My husband was very ill that year.' Cả hai sự việc xảy ra trong cùng một quãng thời gian. Quá khứ đơn cho phép so sánh hai 'thực tại' song song.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The dialogue uses past simple for two parallel timelines: 'You played a sad piece every Friday' (Hoa's side of the wall) and 'My husband was very ill that year' (Mrs Phuong's side). Past simple lets us place two timelines beside each other without complex tenses.

translationVi:
Bà Phương: Xin lỗi. Cháu có phải cô gái hay chơi đàn piano không?
Hoa: Vâng. Cháu xin lỗi. Cháu chơi to quá ạ?
Bà Phương: Không. Bà tới hỏi một câu thôi.
Hoa: Vâng bà hỏi đi ạ.
Bà Phương: Hai năm trước, mỗi tối thứ Sáu cháu chơi một bản nhạc buồn. Rồi cháu dừng. Vì sao?
Hoa: Bà nghe được sao?
Bà Phương: Thứ Sáu nào cũng nghe. Qua tường.
Hoa: Đó là bản nhạc bà cháu rất thích. Bà cháu mất mùa hè đó. Cháu chơi mỗi tối thứ Sáu suốt một năm. Rồi cháu dừng.
Bà Phương: Bà xin lỗi cháu.
Hoa: Vì sao bà muốn biết ạ?
Bà Phương: Chồng bà ốm rất nặng năm đó. Vợ chồng bà không ngủ được. Mỗi tối thứ Sáu, bản nhạc của cháu là thứ yên bình duy nhất trong căn hộ. Ông ấy chờ nó.
Hoa: Cháu không biết.
Bà Phương: Khi cháu dừng, ông ấy hỏi bà: 'Sao cô bé dừng vậy?' Bà nói: 'Tôi không biết.' Ông ấy mất tháng Mười.
Hoa: Cháu rất tiếc.
Bà Phương: Hoa à, cháu không cần phải chơi lại đâu. Bà chỉ muốn nói với cháu thôi. Tường mỏng lắm. Vợ chồng bà nghe rõ. Tối thứ Sáu, ông ấy không cô đơn.
Hoa: Bà Phương, cháu hỏi một câu được không ạ?
Bà Phương: Cháu cứ hỏi.
Hoa: Bà vào nhà cháu một lát được không ạ? Cháu sẽ chơi lại cho bà nghe. Một lần thôi.
Bà Phương: Được. Cảm ơn cháu.

questions.prompt: Why did Hoa stop playing the Friday piece?
options: Her grandmother died that summer, and she played it for a year then stopped.
She moved to a different flat.
questions.explanation: 'It was a piece my grandmother loved. She died that summer. I played it every Friday for one year. Then I stopped.'

questions.prompt: Why does Mrs Phuong knock on Hoa's door?
options: To ask Hoa to stop playing
To ask why Hoa stopped playing the Friday piece
questions.explanation: 'Two years ago, you played a sad piece every Friday night. Then you stopped. Why?'

questions.prompt: What was happening in Mrs Phuong's flat at that time?
options: Her husband was very ill, and they could not sleep.
Her children were small and noisy.
questions.explanation: 'My husband was very ill that year. We could not sleep.'
questions.hint: Mrs Phuong's reply after Hoa asks why she wanted to know.

questions.prompt: What did the piano piece mean to Mrs Phuong's husband?
options: It was the only quiet thing in their flat; he waited for it.
He wanted to learn to play it.
It reminded him of his job.
questions.explanation: 'On Friday nights, your piece was the only quiet thing in our flat. He waited for it.'
questions.hint: Mrs Phuong describes his weekly wait.

questions.prompt: What did Mrs Phuong's husband ask her when Hoa stopped playing?
questions.explanation: 'He asked me, "Why did the girl stop?"'
questions.hint: Mrs Phuong remembers her husband's exact words.

questions.prompt: When did Mrs Phuong's husband die?
Does Mrs Phuong ask Hoa to play the piece again?
options: No. She says Hoa does not need to play it again.
She says Hoa should never play again.
questions.explanation: 'You do not need to play it again. I only wanted to tell you.'
questions.hint: Mrs Phuong is clear: she is not asking for it.

questions.prompt: What does Hoa offer at the end?
options: To play the piece for Mrs Phuong, just once
To teach Mrs Phuong the piano
questions.explanation: 'Could you come in for a moment? I will play it for you. Just once.'

questions.prompt: What does 'The walls are thin' mean here?
options: The walls are old and dangerous.
Sound passes through the walls easily — they heard everything.
The walls are made of paper.
questions.explanation: Mrs Phuong is explaining how they could hear the music — the walls let the sound through.
questions.hint: Look at the sentence right after: 'We heard you.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Something we did privately can quietly matter to people we have never met.
Pianos are too loud for apartments.
Music is more useful than medicine.
questions.explanation: Hoa was playing for herself and her grandmother's memory — she had no idea anyone else was listening or that it mattered to them.
questions.hint: Think about what Hoa learns from this conversation.

cloze.template:
Mrs Phuong: Excuse me. Are you the girl who plays the {{b1}}?
Hoa: Yes. I am very sorry. Was I too loud?
Mrs Phuong: No. I came to ask a {{b2}}.
Hoa: Please.
Mrs Phuong: Two years ago, you played a sad piece every Friday night. Then you {{b3}}. Why?
Hoa: You heard that?
Mrs Phuong: Every Friday. Through the {{b4}}.
Hoa: It was a piece my grandmother loved. She died that summer. I played it every Friday for one year. Then I stopped.
Mrs Phuong: I am sorry.
Hoa: Why did you want to know?
Mrs Phuong: My husband was very {{b5}} that year. We could not sleep. On Friday nights, your piece was the only {{b6}} thing in our flat. He waited for it.
Hoa: I did not know.
Mrs Phuong: When you stopped, he asked me, 'Why did the girl stop?' I said, 'I do not know.' He died in {{b7}}.
Hoa: I am so sorry.
Mrs Phuong: Hoa, you do not need to play it again. I only wanted to tell you. The walls are {{b8}}. We heard you. He was not {{b9}} on Friday nights.
Hoa: Mrs Phuong, can I ask one thing?
Mrs Phuong: Of course.
Hoa: Could you come in for a moment? I will play it for you. Just {{b10}}.
Mrs Phuong: Yes. Please.

blanks.explanation: Mrs Phuong asks about the piano.
She came to ask a question.
Then Hoa stopped playing the piece.
Through the wall — between the two flats.
It was the only quiet thing in the flat.
He was not alone on Friday nights — because of the music.
Hoa offers to play it just once.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Hoa thought she was playing only for herself and her grandmother. Mrs Phuong's husband was listening through the wall. Is there a 'private' thing you do — a song, a habit, an evening walk — that other people might quietly notice? Would you do it differently if you knew?
